summary: Iteration 12: Job Titles feature and Settings Deploy tab enhancements fully tested. Backend: 13/13 tests passed. Frontend: All UI flows verified - Job Titles button visible, dialog shows 15 default titles, CRUD works, Job Title dropdown in Add Employee form with salary auto-fill, Deploy tab shows GoDaddy-specific instructions, Railway (Recommended), VPS, Render options, and PWA install guide.

issues: Console warning: React hydration error - <tr> cannot be child of <span> and <span> cannot be child of <tbody>. Cosmetic issue, does not affect functionality.

passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/job-titles returns list with 15+ default titles
Backend: Job titles have title, department, min_salary, max_salary fields
Backend: Default titles include Chef, Cashier, Manager, Waiter, Driver, Sous Chef, Line Cook, Supervisor
Backend: POST /api/job-titles creates new title successfully
Backend: DELETE /api/job-titles/{id} removes title
Backend: Employee model supports job_title_id field
Backend: GET /api/settings/company, /api/settings/email, /api/settings/whatsapp endpoints work
Frontend: Login with SSC@SSC.com / Aa147258369SsC@ works
Frontend: Employees page shows 'Job Titles' button in header

Frontend: Job Titles dialog shows all 15 default titles (Chef, Sous Chef, Line Cook, Cashier, Waiter, Manager, Supervisor, Driver, Cleaner, Accountant, Delivery, Security, Kitchen Helper, Receptionist, Barista)
Frontend: Job Titles dialog has Title, Department, Salary Range, Employees, Actions columns
Frontend: Add new job title flow works - added 'Dishwasher' and saw success toast
Frontend: Delete job title works
Frontend: Add Employee dialog has Job Title dropdown instead of free-text position field
Frontend: Job Title dropdown shows all titles with department (e.g., 'Chef (Kitchen)')
Frontend: Selecting job title auto-fills salary with min_salary (Chef -> 2000)
Frontend: Employees table has 'Job Title' column header
Frontend: Settings Deploy tab shows Railway.app with 'Recommended' badge
Frontend: Deploy tab shows GoDaddy VPS / Any VPS option
Frontend: Deploy tab shows Render.com option
Frontend: Deploy tab has GoDaddy Domain Setup section with CNAME and A record instructions
Frontend: Deploy tab shows Environment Variables including TWILIO and EMERGENT_LLM_KEY
Frontend: Deploy tab has 'Install App on Phones (PWA)' section
Frontend: PWA section has Android (Chrome) instructions with 3 dots menu, Add to Home screen
Frontend: PWA section has iPhone (Safari) instructions with Share button, Add to Home Screen
critical_code_review_comments: Job titles auto-seed 15 default titles on first GET request - working correctly
Job title CRUD endpoints correctly implemented (GET, POST, DELETE)
Employee form Job Title dropdown correctly filters active job titles
Salary auto-fill when selecting job title works when salary field is empty
Deploy tab content is comprehensive with Railway, VPS, Render options
GoDaddy-specific DNS instructions clearly differentiate CNAME (for Railway/Render) vs A record (for VPS)
PWA install instructions cover both Android (Chrome 3-dot menu) and iPhone (Safari share button)
React hydration warning (span/tr/tbody nesting) is cosmetic and does not affect functionality
seed_data_creation: 15 default job titles auto-seeded: Chef, Sous Chef, Line Cook, Cashier, Waiter, Manager, Supervisor, Driver, Cleaner, Accountant, Delivery, Security, Kitchen Helper, Receptionist, Barista. Test job title 'Dishwasher' created and deleted during testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 12 complete. Job Titles feature fully implemented and tested. Key findings: 1) 15 default job titles auto-seed on first GET /api/job-titles call. 2) Job titles have title, department, min_salary, max_salary fields. 3) Employee form now has Job Title dropdown instead of free-text position. 4) Selecting a job title auto-fills salary with min_salary when salary field is empty. 5) Settings Deploy tab enhanced with Railway (Recommended), VPS, Render options, GoDaddy DNS setup (CNAME/A record), and PWA install guide for Android/iPhone. 6) Environment variables section includes TWILIO and EMERGENT_LLM_KEY variables.
